legal_basis: Finance Act 2013 Schedule 36; International Tax Compliance Regulations 2015 (SI 2015/878) as amended
crs_status: Committed and active - first exchange 2017 (early adopter)
reporting_deadline: 31 May following the 31 December reporting year-end
submission_method: HMRC AEOI online service via Government Gateway; XML upload
xml_schema: Combined CRS+FATCA XML schema (HMRC format) until 31 December 2026; from 1 January 2027: OECD CRS XML v3.0 (OECD October 2024) for CRS + IRS FATCA XML for FATCA
nil_report_required: Not required unless FI has made a threshold election for de minimis
data_retention_years: 6 years from end of the reporting period
msg_ref_id_format: FI TIN + Year + Sequence - HMRC specifies format in AEOI technical guidance
doc_ref_id_format: Must be globally unique per account per FI; HMRC recommends: [CountryCode][FI_TIN][Year][AccountRef]
character_encoding: ISO-8859-1 (Latin characters only; extended Unicode characters must be transliterated)
local_fi_scope: UK-resident banks, building societies, credit unions
UK-resident life insurance companies with investment-linked products
UK-resident collective investment vehicles (OEICs, unit trusts, etc.)
Foreign branches of non-UK entities resident in UK for CRS purposes
excluded_accounts: Qualified credit card issuer accounts (where applicable)
Individual Savings Accounts (ISAs) - exempt as low-risk
Dormant accounts: accounts with no activity for 3+ years and no communication for 6+ years (FI may elect not to review, but must report if identified as reportable)

key_dates.schema_change_date: 1 January 2027 - switch to separate OECD CRS XML v3.0 and IRS FATCA XML

local_specific_rules: SCHEMA TRANSITION: Until 31 December 2026, HMRC accepts a single combined XML file covering both CRS and FATCA obligations. From 1 January 2027, these must be filed separately: OECD CRS XML Schema v3.0 (published October 2024) for CRS, and IRS FATCA XML for FATCA.

DORMANT ACCOUNT ELECTION: FI may elect dormant account treatment if account has had no transactions for 3+ calendar years AND no communication with account holder for 6+ calendar years. Once dormant, FI does not need to actively review, but must report if account holder is later identified as reportable.

UNDOCUMENTED ACCOUNTS: If FI holds only a hold-mail or in-care-of address for an account holder with no other address on file, HMRC guidance requires the account to be treated as UK resident and reported as such.
CHARACTER SET: Only ISO-8859-1 characters are accepted. Names with diacritics, Arabic, Chinese, or other non-Latin characters must be transliterated to Latin equivalents.
NIL REPORTING: UK does not require nil reports for CRS. However, if FI has previously reported and now has zero reportable accounts, HMRC recommends confirming this via the portal.
CLASSIFICATION OF PENSIONS: Most UK regulated pension schemes are Non-Reporting FIs under HMRC guidance. FIs managing pension assets must verify the pension fund's classification before applying CRS.
THRESHOLD ELECTION: UK allows FI to elect to apply a USD 50,000 de minimis threshold for pre-existing individual accounts. Once elected, accounts below threshold need not be reviewed.
GOVERNMENT GATEWAY: Mandatory for all AEOI submissions. Each Reporting FI must have its own Government Gateway credential linked to its UTR or company registration.

rejection_error_codes.resolution: Check submission history; use OECD2 with CorrDocRefId to amend

rejection_error_codes.desc: CorrDocRefId not found in HMRC systems
rejection_error_codes.resolution: Verify original was accepted; resubmit original as OECD1 if not found

rejection_error_codes.desc: Schema validation error - mandatory element missing
rejection_error_codes.resolution: Validate XML against published XSD before submission
Use ISO 3166-1 alpha-2; verify against HMRC list of participating jurisdictions
Verify FI is registered with HMRC for AEOI and UTR matches registration
Include TIN type indicator and validate against OECD TIN issuance rules

rejection_error_codes.desc: File too large for single submission
rejection_error_codes.resolution: Split into multiple files, each with unique MessageRefId

penalties.failure_to_report: Up to GBP 3,000 per year of non-compliance; daily penalties possible
penalties.inaccurate_return: GBP 300 per inaccuracy for careless errors; higher for deliberate
penalties.failure_to_comply_with_notice: GBP 300 + up to GBP 60 per day

fatca.portal: Same HMRC AEOI Online Service until Dec 2026; IRS IDES from Jan 2027 for FATCA-specific filing
key_rules: UK Model 1A: UK FIs report FATCA data to HMRC, which passes to IRS. FIs do NOT register on IRS FATCA portal as FFIs.
US indicia: 7 standard indicia apply. Enhanced review required for high-value accounts.
Combined filing until 31 December 2026: single XML covers CRS + FATCA obligations.
From 1 January 2027: FATCA filed separately using IRS schema; CRS filed using OECD v3.0.

sources.notes: HMRC AEOI main guidance page - fetched and verified

gaps: HMRC rejection error codes use internal naming; should be cross-checked against latest HMRC AEOI technical specs
Penalty amounts should be verified against Finance Act amendments post-2024
